Definition.text: Aura is a skill or buff that applies effects on nearby allies and/or enemies within the aura area of effect.

Purpose.text: Attach persistent effect delivery to nearby entities while the source remains active and resource-reservation is satisfied.

Core Rules.text: Aura is a subset of buff/debuff and inherits that attachment model. It can be positive or negative and can be temporary or permanent.

Internal Rules.text: Aura effects can only refresh affected targets every 250ms. Aura effect values shown in tooltips are base values and do not include all increases.

Calculation Order.text: 1) determine valid aura source 2) reserve required resource 3) create aura instance 4) apply aura state to valid targets in radius every 250ms 5) track lifetime and remove on expiry/loss conditions.

Priority Rules.text: Friendly auras grant radius-based beneficiary state from the source. Curse-related aura behavior and non-curse aura effect scaling can be separated by source modifiers.

Preconditions.text: Auras require a valid aura source and resource reservation state; Blasphemy and similar delivery methods require the same source constraints.

Postconditions.text: Targets within aura radius receive the aura effects while the aura instance remains valid.

Limitations.text: Aura radius and target visibility rules are constrained by the aura area of effect and source restrictions.

Caps.text: No single universal aura cap is encoded in this term alone; cap behavior depends on source, socketing, reservation, and curse/other framework interactions.

Formula.text: No single canonical formula applies to all auras; each aura source has its own base values then applies resource and effect scaling.

Conflicts.text: Aura behavior conflicts can occur with reserve-efficiency changes and map/monster modifiers that alter normal application rules.

Exceptions.text: Auras can be supported by aura-specific supports and may convert mana reservation into skill cost through explicit support effects.

Hidden Rules.text: Support gems can alter what is reserved (mana vs life) without changing base aura effect math unless explicitly stated.

Developer Notes.text: Treat Aura as a buff/debuff subtype with update-frequency and source-constraint behavior as first-class mechanics, not only a tooltip category.

Historical Changes.text: Aura has been modified through aura-quantity and reservation-focused balance updates over multiple patches; specific entries are maintained in patch note sources when available.

Patch History.text: No versioned aura-rule sequence was included in the source snapshot used for this pass; patch-level details are deferred to linked official patch sources.

text: Grace applies an aura to you and nearby allies.
Blasphemy converts supported curses into aura-delivered curse effects.
Aura is not the same as Curse limit state alone.
Aura tooltips do not always show final amplified values.

prevents: instant arbitrary target jumps while out of radius
overrides: previous weaker duplicate aura instances when source rules replace them
removes: old aura states when source no longer valid
